instruction: Build a TypeScript form validator using regex with named groups, lookbehind assertions, and Unicode property escapes that validates international input formats including emails, phone numbers, and postal codes.
output:
<think>
Modern JavaScript/TypeScript regex supports:
1. Named groups: `(?<name>pattern)` (ES2018)
2. Lookbehind: `(?<=...)` and `(?<!...)` (ES2018)
3. Unicode property escapes: `\p{Script=Latin}`, `\p{L}` (ES2018, with /u flag)
4. The /v flag (ES2024) for set notation and string properties

For international validation:
- Emails: Unicode local parts are technically valid (RFC 6531)
- Phone numbers: various international formats (+1-xxx, +44 xxx, etc.)
- Postal codes: different formats per country
- Names: must accept Unicode letters (accents, CJK, etc.)

I'll build a validator class with typed validation rules using these features. Each rule has a regex with named groups for structured error messages.

Lookbehind is useful for: password strength (digit not preceded by another digit = spread out), checking format without consuming characters.
</think>

```typescript
interface ValidationResult {
  valid: boolean;
  field: string;
  value: string;
  errors: string[];
  groups?: Record<string, string>;
}

interface ValidationRule {
  name: string;
  pattern: RegExp;
  message: string;
  extract?: (groups: Record<string, string>) => Record<string, string>;
}

class InternationalValidator {
  private rules = new Map<string, ValidationRule[]>();

  constructor() {
    this.registerDefaults();
  }

  private registerDefaults(): void {
    // Email with Unicode support and named groups
    this.addRule('email', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<local>[\w.\p{L}+-]+)@(?<domain>[\w\p{L}-]+\.(?<tld>[\p{L}]{2,}))$/u,
      message: 'Invalid email format',
      extract: (g) => ({ local: g.local, domain: g.domain, tld: g.tld }),
    });

    // International phone with named groups for country code
    this.addRule('phone', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<prefix>\+?(?<country>\d{1,3}))[\s.-]?(?<area>\d{2,4})[\s.-]?(?<number>\d{4,10})$/,
      message: 'Invalid phone format (expected: +1-234-5678901)',
    });

    // Password strength using lookbehind and lookahead
    this.addRule('password', {
      name: 'length',
      pattern: /^.{8,128}$/,
      message: 'Password must be 8-128 characters',
    });
    this.addRule('password', {
      name: 'uppercase',
      pattern: /(?=.*\p{Lu})/u,  // Unicode uppercase letter
      message: 'Password must contain an uppercase letter',
    });
    this.addRule('password', {
      name: 'lowercase',
      pattern: /(?=.*\p{Ll})/u,  // Unicode lowercase letter
      message: 'Password must contain a lowercase letter',
    });
    this.addRule('password', {
      name: 'digit',
      pattern: /(?=.*\d)/,
      message: 'Password must contain a digit',
    });
    this.addRule('password', {
      name: 'special',
      pattern: /(?=.*[^\w\s])/,
      message: 'Password must contain a special character',
    });
    // Lookbehind: no 3+ repeated characters
    this.addRule('password', {
      name: 'no_repeats',
      pattern: /^(?!.*(.)\ 1{2})/,
      message: 'Password must not have 3+ repeated characters',
    });

    // International name (Unicode letters, spaces, hyphens, apostrophes)
    this.addRule('name', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<first>[\p{L}]+(?:[\s'-][\p{L}]+)*)\s+(?<last>[\p{L}]+(?:[\s'-][\p{L}]+)*)$/u,
      message: 'Name must contain first and last name with valid Unicode characters',
      extract: (g) => ({ firstName: g.first, lastName: g.last }),
    });

    // Postal codes by country pattern
    this.addRule('postal_us', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<zip>\d{5})(?:-(?<plus4>\d{4}))?$/,
      message: 'Invalid US ZIP code (expected: 12345 or 12345-6789)',
    });
    this.addRule('postal_uk', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<outward>[A-Z]{1,2}\d[A-Z\d]?)\s*(?<inward>\d[A-Z]{2})$/i,
      message: 'Invalid UK postcode (expected: SW1A 1AA)',
    });
    this.addRule('postal_jp', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<prefix>\d{3})-?(?<suffix>\d{4})$/,
      message: 'Invalid Japanese postal code (expected: 123-4567)',
    });

    // URL with lookbehind for protocol detection
    this.addRule('url', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<protocol>https?):\/\/(?<host>[\w\p{L}.-]+)(?::(?<port>\d{1,5}))?(?<path>\/[^\s]*)?$/u,
      message: 'Invalid URL format',
    });

    // Credit card with Luhn-compatible format (spaces/dashes allowed)
    this.addRule('credit_card', {
      name: 'format',
      pattern: /^(?<issuer>4\d{3}|5[1-5]\d{2}|3[47]\d{2}|6(?:011|5\d{2}))[\s-]?(?<group2>\d{4})[\s-]?(?<group3>\d{4})[\s-]?(?<group4>\d{4})$/,
      message: 'Invalid credit card format',
    });
  }

  addRule(field: string, rule: ValidationRule): void {
    if (!this.rules.has(field)) {
      this.rules.set(field, []);
    }
    this.rules.get(field)!.push(rule);
  }

  validate(field: string, value: string): ValidationResult {
    const rules = this.rules.get(field);
    if (!rules) {
      return { valid: false, field, value, errors: [`No rules for field: ${field}`] };
    }

    const errors: string[] = [];
    let extractedGroups: Record<string, string> = {};

    for (const rule of rules) {
      const match = rule.pattern.exec(value);
      if (!match) {
        errors.push(rule.message);
      } else if (match.groups && rule.extract) {
        Object.assign(extractedGroups, rule.extract(match.groups));
      } else if (match.groups) {
        Object.assign(extractedGroups, match.groups);
      }
    }

    return {
      valid: errors.length === 0,
      field,
      value,
      errors,
      groups: Object.keys(extractedGroups).length > 0 ? extractedGroups : undefined,
    };
  }

  validateAll(data: Record<string, { field: string; value: string }>): ValidationResult[] {
    return Object.values(data).map(({ field, value }) => this.validate(field, value));
  }
}

// Usage
const v = new InternationalValidator();

const tests: Array<[string, string]> = [
  ['email', 'müller@beispiel.de'],
  ['name', 'José García-López'],
  ['phone', '+44-7911-123456'],
  ['postal_uk', 'SW1A 1AA'],
  ['postal_jp', '100-0001'],
  ['password', 'Str0ng!Pass'],
  ['url', 'https://例え.jp/path'],
];

for (const [field, value] of tests) {
  const result = v.validate(field, value);
  console.log(`${field}: "${value}" -> ${result.valid ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'}`);
  if (result.groups) console.log(`  groups: ${JSON.stringify(result.groups)}`);
  if (!result.valid) console.log(`  errors: ${result.errors.join(', ')}`);
}
```

instruction: Create a TypeScript tagged template literal system that uses regex internally for parsing template expressions, supporting filters, conditionals, and type-safe interpolation.
output:
<think>
Tagged template literals in TypeScript are functions that receive the template string parts and interpolated values. I need to build a system that:
1. Uses regex internally to parse expressions within the template
2. Supports filters: `${value|upper|truncate:20}`
3. Supports conditionals: `${value ? 'yes' : 'no'}`
4. Type-safe interpolation with generics

The tagged template function receives (strings: TemplateStringsArray, ...values: unknown[]). I need to process each value through filters if the preceding string ends with a filter syntax marker.

Actually, with tagged templates, the filters need to be embedded differently. I'll use a two-phase approach:
1. Phase 1: The tagged function processes interpolated values
2. Phase 2: Post-processing with regex to handle inline expressions

Or better: create a template compiler that takes a string with ${expr|filter} syntax and compiles it to a function using regex parsing.
</think>

```typescript
// --- Filter registry ---
type FilterFn = (value: unknown, ...args: string[]) => unknown;

const filters: Record<string, FilterFn> = {
  upper: (v) => String(v).toUpperCase(),
  lower: (v) => String(v).toLowerCase(),
  trim: (v) => String(v).trim(),
  truncate: (v, len = '20') => {
    const s = String(v);
    const n = parseInt(len);
    return s.length > n ? s.slice(0, n) + '...' : s;
  },
  default: (v, fallback = '') => v ?? fallback,
  currency: (v) => `$${Number(v).toFixed(2)}`,
  json: (v) => JSON.stringify(v, null, 2),
  date: (v) => new Date(v as string).toLocaleDateString(),
  plural: (v, singular = '', pluralForm = 's') =>
    `${v} ${singular}${Number(v) === 1 ? '' : pluralForm}`,
};

// --- Regex patterns for template parsing ---
const EXPR_RE = /\$\{(?<expr>[^}]+)\}/g;
const FILTER_RE = /^(?<value>[\w.]+)(?<filters>(?:\|[\w]+(?::[^|]*)?)*)$/;
const SINGLE_FILTER_RE = /\|(?<name>\w+)(?::(?<args>[^|]*))?/g;
const CONDITIONAL_RE = /^(?<cond>[\w.]+)\s*\?\s*(?<truthy>[^:]+)\s*:\s*(?<falsy>.+)$/;
const DOT_PATH_RE = /^[\w]+(?:\.[\w]+)*$/;

// --- Resolve dotted path ---
function resolve(obj: Record<string, unknown>, path: string): unknown {
  return path.split('.').reduce<unknown>(
    (curr, key) => (curr as Record<string, unknown>)?.[key],
    obj
  );
}

// --- Apply filter chain ---
function applyFilters(value: unknown, filterChain: string): unknown {
  let result = value;
  let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
  const re = new RegExp(SINGLE_FILTER_RE.source, SINGLE_FILTER_RE.flags);
  while ((match = re.exec(filterChain)) !== null) {
    const name = match.groups!.name;
    const args = match.groups?.args?.split(',').map(s => s.trim()) ?? [];
    const fn = filters[name];
    if (fn) {
      result = fn(result, ...args);
    } else {
      throw new Error(`Unknown filter: ${name}`);
    }
  }
  return result;
}

// --- Template compiler ---
function compileTemplate(template: string): (data: Record<string, unknown>) => string {
  // Parse and cache expression positions
  const parts: Array<{ type: 'literal'; value: string } | { type: 'expr'; raw: string }> = [];
  let lastIndex = 0;
  let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
  const re = new RegExp(EXPR_RE.source, EXPR_RE.flags);

  while ((match = re.exec(template)) !== null) {
    if (match.index > lastIndex) {
      parts.push({ type: 'literal', value: template.slice(lastIndex, match.index) });
    }
    parts.push({ type: 'expr', raw: match.groups!.expr.trim() });
    lastIndex = re.lastIndex;
  }
  if (lastIndex < template.length) {
    parts.push({ type: 'literal', value: template.slice(lastIndex) });
  }

  return (data: Record<string, unknown>): string => {
    return parts.map(part => {
      if (part.type === 'literal') return part.value;

      const expr = part.raw;

      // Check for conditional: value ? truthy : falsy
      const condMatch = CONDITIONAL_RE.exec(expr);
      if (condMatch) {
        const cond = resolve(data, condMatch.groups!.cond);
        return String(cond ? condMatch.groups!.truthy.trim() : condMatch.groups!.falsy.trim());
      }

      // Check for value with filters: name|upper|truncate:20
      const filterMatch = FILTER_RE.exec(expr);
      if (filterMatch) {
        const value = resolve(data, filterMatch.groups!.value);
        const filterChain = filterMatch.groups!.filters;
        if (filterChain) {
          return String(applyFilters(value, filterChain));
        }
        return String(value ?? '');
      }

      // Plain path
      if (DOT_PATH_RE.test(expr)) {
        return String(resolve(data, expr) ?? '');
      }

      return `[invalid: ${expr}]`;
    }).join('');
  };
}

// --- Tagged template literal (type-safe) ---
function tmpl<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(
  strings: TemplateStringsArray,
  ...keys: Array<keyof T & string>
): (data: T) => string {
  return (data: T) => {
    let result = '';
    strings.forEach((str, i) => {
      result += str;
      if (i < keys.length) {
        result += String(data[keys[i]] ?? '');
      }
    });
    return result;
  };
}

// --- Usage ---

// Compiled template with filters and conditionals
const invoice = compileTemplate(
  `Invoice for \${name|upper}
Email: \${email|lower}
Amount: \${amount|currency}
Status: \${paid ? Paid : Pending}
Items: \${itemCount|plural:item}
Notes: \${notes|default:No notes|truncate:50}`
);

console.log(invoice({
  name: 'John Smith',
  email: 'JOHN@EXAMPLE.COM',
  amount: 1250.5,
  paid: true,
  itemCount: 3,
  notes: 'Please deliver before Friday. This is a very long note that should be truncated.',
}));

// Type-safe tagged template
interface User { name: string; role: string; score: number }
const greeting = tmpl<User>`Hello ${'name'}, your role is ${'role'}`;
console.log(greeting({ name: 'Alice', role: 'admin', score: 95 }));
```

instruction: Implement a TypeScript regex-based Markdown parser that handles inline formatting, links, code blocks, and nested formatting with proper precedence using named groups and non-greedy matching.
output: A Markdown parser using regex needs to handle: 1. Headers: `# H1` through `###### H6` 2. Bold: `**text**` or `__text__` 3. Italic: `*text*` or `_text_` 4. Code: `` `code` `` 5. Links: `[text](url)` 6. Images: `![alt](url)` 7. Code blocks: ``` ```lang\n...\n``` ``` 8. Blockquotes: `> text` 9. Lists: `- item` or `1. item` Precedence matters: code blocks should be processed first (no formatting inside), then inline code, then links, then bold/italic. Non-greedy matching is critical: `**bold** and **bold**` should match two separate bold spans, not one spanning the entire text. Named groups help with extracting components like link text and URL. ```typescript interface MarkdownToken { type: string; raw: string; content?: string; children?: MarkdownToken[]; props?: Record; } class MarkdownParser { // Patterns ordered by precedence (processed first = highest priority) // Code blocks must be matched before inline patterns // Block-level patterns (multiline) private static CODE_BLOCK = /```(?\w*)\n(?[\s\S]*?)\n```/g; private static HEADER = /^(?#{1,6})\s+(?.+)$/gm; private static BLOCKQUOTE = /^>\s+(?.+)$/gm; private static HR = /^(?:---|\.{3}|\*{3})$/gm; private static UL_ITEM = /^[\s]*[-*+]\s+(?.+)$/gm; private static OL_ITEM = /^[\s]*(?\d+)\.\s+(?.+)$/gm; // Inline patterns (non-greedy for proper nesting) private static INLINE_CODE = /`(?[^`]+)`/g; private static IMAGE = /!\[(?[^\]]*)\]\((?[^)]+?)(?:\s+"(?[^"]+)")?\)/g; private static LINK = /\[(?<text>[^\]]+)\]\((?<url>[^)]+?)(?:\s+"(?<title>[^"]+)")?\)/g; private static BOLD_STAR = /\*\*(?<text>[^*]+?)\*\*/g; private static BOLD_UNDER = /__(?<text>[^_]+?)__/g; private static ITALIC_STAR = /(?<!\*)\*(?<text>[^*]+?)\*(?!\*)/g; private static ITALIC_UNDER = /(?<!_)_(?<text>[^_]+?)_(?!_)/g; private static STRIKETHROUGH = /~~(?<text>.+?)~~/g; // HTML entity safety private static escapeHtml(text: string): string { return text .replace(/&/g, '&') .replace(/</g, '<') .replace(/>/g, '>') .replace(/"/g, '"'); } /** * Convert Markdown to HTML with proper precedence. */ toHtml(markdown: string): string { let html = markdown; // Phase 1: Extract and protect code blocks (no formatting inside) const codeBlocks: string[] = []; html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.CODE_BLOCK, (_match, lang, code) => { const idx = codeBlocks.length; const escaped = MarkdownParser.escapeHtml(code); const langAttr = lang ? ` class="language-${lang}"` : ''; codeBlocks.push(`<pre><code${langAttr}>${escaped}</code></pre>`); return `\x00CB${idx}\x00`; }); // Phase 2: Extract inline code const inlineCodes: string[] = []; html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.INLINE_CODE, (_match, code) => { const idx = inlineCodes.length; inlineCodes.push(`<code>${MarkdownParser.escapeHtml(code)}</code>`); return `\x00IC${idx}\x00`; }); // Phase 3: Block-level elements html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.HEADER, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; const level = groups.hashes.length; return `<h${level}>${groups.text}</h${level}>`; }); html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.HR, '<hr>'); html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.BLOCKQUOTE, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; return `<blockquote>${groups.text}</blockquote>`; }); // Phase 4: Inline elements (order matters for nesting) html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.IMAGE, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; const title = groups.title ? ` title="${groups.title}"` : ''; return `<img src="${groups.url}" alt="${groups.alt}"${title}>`; }); html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.LINK, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; const title = groups.title ? ` title="${groups.title}"` : ''; return `<a href="${groups.url}"${title}>${groups.text}</a>`; }); html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.STRIKETHROUGH, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; return `<del>${groups.text}</del>`; }); // Bold before italic (** before *) html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.BOLD_STAR, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; return `<strong>${groups.text}</strong>`; }); html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.BOLD_UNDER, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; return `<strong>${groups.text}</strong>`; }); html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.ITALIC_STAR, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; return `<em>${groups.text}</em>`; }); html = html.replace(MarkdownParser.ITALIC_UNDER, (...args) => { const groups = args[args.length - 1] as Record<string, string>; return `<em>${groups.text}</em>`; }); // Phase 5: Paragraphs (consecutive non-empty, non-block lines) html = html.replace(/^(?!<[a-z]|\x00)(.+)$/gm, '<p>$1</p>'); // Phase 6: Restore protected elements html = html.replace(/\x00IC(\d+)\x00/g, (_, idx) => inlineCodes[parseInt(idx)]); html = html.replace(/\x00CB(\d+)\x00/g, (_, idx) => codeBlocks[parseInt(idx)]); return html.trim(); } /** * Extract structured tokens for AST-based processing. */ tokenize(markdown: string): MarkdownToken[] { const tokens: MarkdownToken[] = []; const lines = markdown.split('\n'); for (const line of lines) { const headerMatch = /^(?<hashes>#{1,6})\s+(?<text>.+)$/.exec(line); if (headerMatch?.groups) { tokens.push({ type: `h${headerMatch.groups.hashes.length}`, raw: line, content: headerMatch.groups.text, }); continue; } if (line.trim()) { tokens.push({ type: 'text', raw: line, content: line }); } } return tokens; } } // Usage const parser = new MarkdownParser(); const md = `# Hello World This is **bold** and *italic* and ~~struck~~. Here is \`inline code\` and a [link](https://example.com "Example"). > A wise blockquote \`\`\`typescript const x: number = 42; console.log(x); \`\`\` ![Logo](logo.png "Our Logo") `; console.log(parser.toHtml(md)); ```

instruction: Build a TypeScript regex-based code syntax highlighter that uses named capture groups to identify language tokens (keywords, strings, numbers, comments) and produces HTML with proper CSS class annotations.
output:
A syntax highlighter needs to: 1. Identify different token types: keywords, strings, numbers, comments, operators, identifiers 2. Use regex with named groups to classify each token 3. Process tokens in priority order (comments and strings first, since they can contain keywords) 4. Produce HTML spans with CSS classes 5. Handle multiple languages The approach: - Define language grammars as sets of regex patterns with named groups - Process text by finding the earliest match across all patterns - Handle overlapping by consuming matched text (advance past the match) - Escape HTML entities in non-token text Key: comments and strings must be matched first because they take priority. A keyword inside a comment is not a keyword. ```typescript interface TokenPattern { name: string; pattern: RegExp; cssClass: string; } interface LanguageGrammar { name: string; patterns: TokenPattern[]; // Ordered by priority } interface Token { type: string; value: string; start: number; end: number; } class SyntaxHighlighter { private grammars = new Map(); constructor() { this.registerTypeScript(); this.registerPython(); this.registerRust(); } private registerTypeScript(): void { this.grammars.set('typescript', { name: 'TypeScript', patterns: [ // Comments first (highest priority) { name: 'comment-block', pattern: /\/\*[\s\S]*?\*\//g, cssClass: 'hljs-comment' }, { name: 'comment-line', pattern: /\/\/.*$/gm, cssClass: 'hljs-comment' }, // Strings (before keywords) { name: 'string-template', pattern: /`(?:[^`\\]|\\.)*`/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'string-double', pattern: /"(?:[^"\\]|\\.)*"/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'string-single', pattern: /'(?:[^'\\]|\\.)*'/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, // Types { name: 'type', pattern: /\b(?:string|number|boolean|void|never|any|unknown|null|undefined|object|bigint|symbol)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-type' }, // Keywords { name: 'keyword', pattern: /\b(?:const|let|var|function|return|if|else|for|while|do|switch|case|break|continue|class|interface|type|enum|extends|implements|import|export|from|default|async|await|try|catch|finally|throw|new|delete|typeof|instanceof|in|of|as|is|keyof|readonly|public|private|protected|static|abstract|override|satisfies)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-keyword' }, // Built-in values { name: 'literal', pattern: /\b(?:true|false|null|undefined|NaN|Infinity|this|super)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-literal' }, // Numbers { name: 'number', pattern: /\b(?:0x[\da-fA-F]+|0b[01]+|0o[0-7]+|\d+(?:\.\d+)?(?:[eE][+-]?\d+)?n?)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-number' }, // Function calls { name: 'function', pattern: /(?[\w$]+)(?=\s*[<(])/g, cssClass: 'hljs-function' }, // Decorators { name: 'decorator', pattern: /@[\w$]+/g, cssClass: 'hljs-decorator' }, // Operators { name: 'operator', pattern: /[+\-*/%=<>!&|^~?:]+|=>/g, cssClass: 'hljs-operator' }, // Punctuation { name: 'punctuation', pattern: /[{}()\[\];,.]/g, cssClass: 'hljs-punctuation' }, ], }); } private registerPython(): void { this.grammars.set('python', { name: 'Python', patterns: [ { name: 'comment', pattern: /#.*$/gm, cssClass: 'hljs-comment' }, { name: 'string-triple-d', pattern: /"""[\s\S]*?"""/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'string-triple-s', pattern: /'''[\s\S]*?'''/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'string-f', pattern: /f["'](?:[^"'\\]|\\.)*["']/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'string', pattern: /["'](?:[^"'\\]|\\.)*["']/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'keyword', pattern: /\b(?:def|class|return|if|elif|else|for|while|with|as|import|from|try|except|finally|raise|yield|async|await|lambda|pass|break|continue|and|or|not|in|is|global|nonlocal|assert|del)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-keyword' }, { name: 'builtin', pattern: /\b(?:print|len|range|int|str|list|dict|set|tuple|type|isinstance|getattr|setattr|hasattr|super|property|classmethod|staticmethod|enumerate|zip|map|filter)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-built_in' }, { name: 'literal', pattern: /\b(?:True|False|None)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-literal' }, { name: 'decorator', pattern: /@[\w.]+/g, cssClass: 'hljs-decorator' }, { name: 'number', pattern: /\b(?:0x[\da-fA-F]+|0b[01]+|0o[0-7]+|\d+(?:\.\d+)?(?:[eE][+-]?\d+)?j?)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-number' }, { name: 'function', pattern: /(?<=def\s+)(?\w+)/g, cssClass: 'hljs-function' }, ], }); } private registerRust(): void { this.grammars.set('rust', { name: 'Rust', patterns: [ { name: 'comment-block', pattern: /\/\*[\s\S]*?\*\//g, cssClass: 'hljs-comment' }, { name: 'comment-line', pattern: /\/\/.*$/gm, cssClass: 'hljs-comment' }, { name: 'string', pattern: /"(?:[^"\\]|\\.)*"/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'char', pattern: /'(?:[^'\\]|\\.)'/g, cssClass: 'hljs-string' }, { name: 'keyword', pattern: /\b(?:fn|let|mut|const|if|else|match|for|while|loop|return|struct|enum|impl|trait|pub|use|mod|crate|self|super|async|await|move|ref|where|type|as|in|unsafe|extern|dyn|static|break|continue)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-keyword' }, { name: 'type', pattern: /\b(?:i8|i16|i32|i64|i128|isize|u8|u16|u32|u64|u128|usize|f32|f64|bool|char|str|String|Vec|Option|Result|Box|Rc|Arc|HashMap|HashSet|Self)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-type' }, { name: 'literal', pattern: /\b(?:true|false|None|Some|Ok|Err)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-literal' }, { name: 'macro', pattern: /\b\w+!/g, cssClass: 'hljs-macro' }, { name: 'number', pattern: /\b(?:0x[\da-fA-F_]+|0b[01_]+|0o[0-7_]+|\d[\d_]*(?:\.\d[\d_]*)?(?:[eE][+-]?\d+)?(?:i8|i16|i32|i64|u8|u16|u32|u64|f32|f64|isize|usize)?)\b/g, cssClass: 'hljs-number' }, { name: 'lifetime', pattern: /'[a-zA-Z_]\w*/g, cssClass: 'hljs-symbol' }, { name: 'attribute', pattern: /#!?\[[^\]]*\]/g, cssClass: 'hljs-meta' }, ], }); } private escapeHtml(text: string): string { return text.replace(/&/g, '&').replace(//g, '>'); } highlight(code: string, language: string): string { const grammar = this.grammars.get(language); if (!grammar) return `
${this.escapeHtml(code)}
`; // Collect all token matches with positions const tokens: Token[] = []; for (const pat of grammar.patterns) { const re = new RegExp(pat.pattern.source, pat.pattern.flags); let match: RegExpExecArray | null; while ((match = re.exec(code)) !== null) { tokens.push({ type: pat.cssClass, value: match[0], start: match.index, end: match.index + match[0].length, }); } } // Sort by start position, then by priority (earlier in patterns = higher) tokens.sort((a, b) => a.start - b.start || a.end - b.end); // Remove overlapping tokens (first match wins) const filtered: Token[] = []; let lastEnd = 0; for (const token of tokens) { if (token.start >= lastEnd) { filtered.push(token); lastEnd = token.end; } } // Build HTML let html = ''; let pos = 0; for (const token of filtered) { if (token.start > pos) { html += this.escapeHtml(code.slice(pos, token.start)); } html += `${this.escapeHtml(token.value)}`; pos = token.end; } if (pos < code.length) { html += this.escapeHtml(code.slice(pos)); } return `
${html}
`; } } const hl = new SyntaxHighlighter(); const tsCode = ` async function fetchUsers(limit: number = 10): Promise { // Fetch from API const response = await fetch(\`/api/users?limit=\${limit}\`); if (!response.ok) throw new Error("Failed"); return response.json(); } `; console.log(hl.highlight(tsCode.trim(), 'typescript')); ```
